user_message:
<task> Review the following proposed addition to /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/CLAUDE.md. The project uses Claude Code Review CI which runs a code-review plugin on every PR. Plugin Agent #1 audits changes against CLAUDE.md. Currently CLAUDE.md has no code review principles section. The proposed section would be added after the existing ## Code Conventions section. Proposed section: --- ## Code Review Principles Principles for automated code review (CI and agent review alike). ### Grounding - Every finding must cite specific code location (file:line) - Do not present inferences as facts; label hypotheses clearly - Ground claims in repository context or tool outputs, not assumptions ### Severity - Categorize findings: Critical (must fix) / Important (should fix) / Suggestion - Critical: correctness bugs, data loss, security vulnerabilities - Important: missing error handling, contract mismatches between docs and code, edge cases - Suggestion: readability, naming, minor optimization ### Depth - After first plausible issue, check second-order failures: empty-state handling, retries, stale state, rollback paths - Verify contract consistency: if CLAUDE.md, docstrings, or specs state X, code must match ### False Positive Avoidance - Do not flag pre-existing issues unrelated to the change - Do not flag issues a linter, typechecker, or CI build step already catches (ruff, pytest, mypy) - Do not flag intentional behavior changes that align with the PRs stated purpose
- Do not flag style preferences not codified in this file or ruff config

### Scope
- Review only the changed code and its immediate blast radius
- Do not suggest unrelated refactors or cleanup
---

Evaluate:
1. Whether these principles accurately capture good code review methodology (especially as applied to an automated LLM-based reviewer)
2. Whether anything important is missing
3. Whether any items should be removed or rephrased

The project is a Python 3.12+ tool (SQLite, Typer CLI, sentence-transformers). It uses ruff for lint/format, pytest for tests, and uv for package management.
</task>

<structured_output_contract>
- Start with a 2-3 sentence overall verdict
- List any Missing principles (title + 1-sentence rationale each)
- List any Problematic items (quote the item + explain the issue + suggest rephrasing if needed)
- List any items that are Accurate and well-stated (brief confirmation only)
- End with a recommended final version of the section, incorporating your changes
</structured_output_contract>

<grounding_rules>
- Ground every critique in how LLM-based automated reviewers actually behave, not general style preferences
- If you flag something as missing, explain why it matters specifically for automated CI review
- Do not suggest additions that would make the reviewer more verbose without clear quality benefit
</grounding_rules>

<default_follow_through_policy>
Complete the full evaluation. Do not stop to ask clarifying questions.
</default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 요청은 `CLAUDE.md`에 추가할 코드 리뷰 원칙 섹션의 적절성을 평가하는 일로 이해했습니다. 먼저 현재 저장된 결정/교훈과 실제 `CLAUDE.md` 문맥을 확인해서, 이 제안이 자동화된 LLM 리뷰어 운영 원칙으로 맞는지 기준부터 고정하겠습니다.
